codeReview.summary: The performance test implementation covers all 3 assertions (VAL-PERF-001, VAL-PERF-002, VAL-PERF-003) with 13 tests. Thresholds (5s page load, 2s session start) are reasonable. However, there are three non-blocking issues: (1) Home page load test includes registration time in measurement, (2) console error filter is overly broad and could mask real API failures, (3) session start timing measurement includes waiting for UI state which may not accurately reflect responsiveness.

issues.description: Home page 'load time' measurement includes registration time. startTime is captured before registerTestUser() call, but the token is needed for auth, so registration must happen first. This means the measured time includes API registration latency (~100-500ms) plus page load, rather than pure page load time. For a more accurate page load test, registration should complete before startTime or the token should be pre-generated.

filterConsoleErrors() filters out 401 and 400 errors which could mask real API failures. While favicon/chrome-extension/ResizeObserver filtering is appropriate, HTTP 401/400 errors during normal usage may indicate bugs (e.g., token expiry, validation failures). In the profile edit test, if the save fails with 400, it would be masked. Consider filtering only specific expected errors or removing these from the filter.

The session start time measurement for 'Begin Meditation shows active session UI within 2 seconds' includes page.waitForTimeout(500) plus the isVisible check itself. The measurement captures endTime right after the isVisible call completes, not when the session actually started being active. The actual session start (button click) response time is obscured by the subsequent waits. More precise timing would capture just the click-to-UI-change delta.

summary: Performance test implementation is solid with good coverage of all 3 assertions and 13 deterministic tests. Thresholds (5s/2s) match requirements. Three non-blocking concerns: Home page test measures registration+load time rather than pure load; console error filter may mask real 401/400 failures; session start timing includes UI wait time. None are blocking as tests are functional and cover the required assertions.
